rulings.Case Title: N&A MEDICAL SUPPLY, INC. VS. SAN BRUNO SKILLED NURSING HOSPITAL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Answers To Form Interrogatories (Set Two) Propounded To San Francisco Nursing Center, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 23, 2019, Line 1, PLAINTIFF N&A MEDICAL SUPPLY, INC.'s Motion For Order Compelling Answers To Form Interrogatories (Set Two) Propounded To San Francisco Nursing Center, Inc. Pro Tem Judge Aaron Minnis, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Denied without prejudice. The motion is moot. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to aaron@minnisandsmallets.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MARTIFER-SILVERADO FUND I, LLC VS. TALESUN SOLAR USA LTD
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Zhongli New Energy Usa Co., Llcs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, August 23, 2019, Line 1, DEFENDANTS TALESUN SOLAR USA LTD AND ZHONGLI NEW ENERGY USA CO., LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant Zhongli New Energy USA Co., LLC's ("ZNE") motion for summary judgment is denied. ZNE fails to maintain its burden of production. ZNE largely relies on the Gu and Sheng declarations to assert that there is no unity of interest between ZNE and Talesun Solar USA Ltd. Their deposition testimonies show that they lack a foundation to testify regarding those issues however, and the court sustains plaintiff's objections to those declarations. Sheng declared that Talesun and ZNE have different ownership. (Sheng Dec., Paras. 4-5, 9.) At his deposition, Sheng admitted that he has no clue about who owned Talesun. (Sheng Depo., 84:2-85:6.) Gu's declaration similarly lacks foundation. At his deposition, he stated that he never heard of ZNE. (Gu Depo., 47:2-12; 68:7-9.) He further testified that he never heard of Talesun and did not work for it. (Gu Depo., 265:8-12; 37:19-24.) A month later Gu signed a declaration as Talesun's person most knowledgeable stating that there is no relationship between ZNE and Talesun. That declaration does not explain how Gu developed such an intimate knowledge of the innerworkings of the two entities in a month. In addition, plaintiff has presented sufficient evidence to create a triable issue of material fact as to whether alter ego liability exists. Sheng now admits that Talesun and ZNE have the same address. (Sheng Depo., 133:4-134:23.) Talesun and ZNE are both in the business of selling solar panels. (Sheng Depo., 48:14-21.) ZNE and Talesun's employees share the same e-mail addresses. (Sheng Depo., 29:9-12.) U.S. customers believed that the two entities were the same as they had the same warehouse. (Danielson Depo., 23:6-11; 30:15-18.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: SECURITY NATIONAL INSURANCE COMPANY VS. MECA BUILDERS INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR ASSIGNMENT OF RIGHTS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, August 23, 2019, Line 4, PLAINTIFF SECURITY NATIONAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR ASSIGNMENT OF RIGHTS. Plaintiff/judgment creditor Security National Insurance Company's CCP Sec. 708.510 motion for an "assignment order re: rights to payment of money due or to become due" to defendant/debtor Meca Builders Inc. is denied. Plaintiff broadly seeks assignment of "the interest, if any of" Meca "in its rights to payment of money due or to become due, whether styled accounts receivable, general intangibles, payment intangibles, accounts, deposit accounts, royalties, fees, commissions, and other rights to money, from its activities as a building contractor." Notwithstanding CCP Sec. 708.510's plain language ("the court may order the judgment debtor to assign"), some courts have ruled that an assignment thereunder may be outright, not simply an order directing the judgment debtor to assign, and the assignment may reach future events. (Ahart, California Practice Guide: Enforcing Judgments and Debts (TRG 2019) Secs. 6:1422.6, 6:1423.) However, as plaintiff concedes, it is required to describe the sources of the right to payment with sufficient detail so defendant can file a claim of exemption or other opposition. This plaintiff does not do. The most liberal cases it cites - Greenbaum v. Islamic Republic of Iran (2008) 782 F.Supp.2d 893, 897 and Philippine Export & Foreign Loan v. Chuidian (1990) 218 Cal.App.3d 1058 - regard arcane issues of international law, and the former specifies five oil companies as sources by name. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: OAK TRUCKING, LLC. VS. JILS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-19-572725 WITH CASE CGC-18-564705
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, August 23, 2019, Line 6, PLAINTIFF OAK TRUCKING, LLC'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTION OF CASE NO. CGC-19-572725 WITH CASE NO. CGC-18-564705. Plaintiff Oak Trucking, LLC's motion to consolidate matters is GRANTED. Under Code of Civil Procedure section 1048, the Court has broad discretion to consolidate actions with common questions of law or fact. (National Elec. Supply Co. v. Mt. Diablo Unified School Dist. (1960) 187 Cal.App.2d 418, 421.) Although Defendant JLIS Inc.'s connection to the bad faith case (No. CGC-19-572725) is tenuous given the conclusory conspiracy allegations, the legal issue of whether the policy issued by defendants Liberty Mutual Insurance Company and Ohio Casualty Insurance Company obligated them to provide coverage for plaintiff Glenn F. Pasiliao's injury and/or defend Oak Trucking is intertwined with the adequacy of coverage procured by JLIS in the broker negligence case (No. CGC-18-564705). The risk of inconsistent rulings on this key issue in the two cases if left unconsolidated outweighs the potential for delay and jury confusion. Defendants have not shown substantial prejudice and judicial economy will be promoted by avoiding duplication of discovery. The cases shall be consolidated for all purposes with case number CGC-18-564705 as the lead case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: MOTIVA ENTERPRISES LLC A DELAWRE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. AXIS SURPLUS INSURANCE COMPANY AN ILLINOIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT - Renewed Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, August 23, 2019, Line 9, DEFENDANT MAXUM INDEMNITY COMPANY'S RENEWED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant Maxum Indemnity Company's motion for summary judgment is granted for the reasons stated in Axis' companion motion. Assuming that plaintiff is an additional insured under the cited policies, no coverage exists based upon the asbestos exclusion. Maxum adopted the asbestos exclusion that was listed in the Axis policy. (UMF 18-20, 36.) The undisputed evidence demonstrates that the injury "arose out of" the asbestos abatement. (UMF 4.) rulings.Case Title: MOTIVA ENTERPRISES LLC A DELAWRE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. AXIS SURPLUS INSURANCE COMPANY AN ILLINOIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Axis And Endurances Notice Of Renewed Joint Motion And Renewed Joint Motion For Summary Judgment (Part 2 Of 2 For Purposes Of Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, August 23, 2019, Line 9, Defendants Axis And Endurance's Renewed Joint Motion For Summary Judgment (Part 2 of 2 for purposes of entry of tentative ruling only). (Continued from Part 1 of 2) "It is settled that this ['arising out of'] language does not import any particular standard of causation or theory of liability into an insurance policy. Rather, it broadly links a factual situation with the event creating liability, and connotes only a minimal causal connection or incidental relationship." (Acceptance Ins. Co. v. Syufy Enterprises (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 321, 328.) In this case, there is more than a minimal causal connection between the injury and the asbestos abatement. The use of the hose was part of the asbestos remediation process and it is irrelevant that the asbestos itself did not directly cause the injury. Given these rulings, the court need not and thus does not address the remaining arguments. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for movants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. (This is the end of the tentative ruling.) =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: MOTIVA ENTERPRISES LLC A DELAWRE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. AXIS SURPLUS INSURANCE COMPANY AN ILLINOIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant National Fire And Marine Insurance Companys Notice Of Motion And Renewed Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, August 23, 2019, Line 9, Defendant National Fire And Marine Insurance Company's Motion and Renewed Motion For Summary Judgment. Defendant National Fire & Marine Insurance Company's motion for summary judgment is granted for the reasons stated in Axis' companion motion. Assuming that plaintiff is an additional insured under the cited policies, no coverage exists based upon the asbestos exclusion. National's policy contains an asbestos exclusion that bars coverage for any bodily injury "arising out of" the removal of asbestos. (UMF 33.) National also adopted the asbestos exclusion that was listed in the Axis policy if it is deemed more restrictive. (UMF 20, 28, 29, 33.) Under either exclusion, plaintiff's claims fail. The undisputed evidence demonstrates that the injury "arose out of" the asbestos abatement. (UMF 40, 41, 61.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for National is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: MOTIVA ENTERPRISES LLC A DELAWRE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. AXIS SURPLUS INSURANCE COMPANY AN ILLINOIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendants Axis And Endurances Notice Of Renewed Joint Motion And Renewed Joint Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, August 23, 2019, Line 9, Defendants Axis And Endurance's Renewed Joint Motion For Summary Judgment (Part 1 of 2 for purposes of entry of tentative ruling only). Defendant Axis Surplus Insurance Company and Endurance American Specialty Insurance Company's motion for summary judgment is granted. Assuming that plaintiff is an additional insured under the cited policies, no coverage exists based upon the asbestos exclusion. Axis' primary policy excludes coverage for bodily injury "arising directly or indirectly, out of…abating…cleaning up, removing...remediation or disposing of, or in any way responding to" asbestos. (UMF 45.) The exclusion "includes defects or negligence in design, construction or materials, or any other event, conduct or misconduct, which may have or is claimed to have precipitated, caused or acted jointly, concurrently, or in any sequence with asbestos in causing injury or damage." (UMF 45.) The Axis and Endurance excess policies adopted the asbestos exclusion. (UMF 51 and 60.) The undisputed facts establish that Mr. McElroy and Mr. Ceaser were injured while abating asbestos. (UMF 74, 75, 80.) Mr. Ceaser used a hose to wash off asbestos fibers, but the hose was tainted with the hydrocarbon naphtha which caused the injurious fire. (UMF 79.) Plaintiff correctly notes that the court narrowly construes policy exclusions. Nevertheless, the term "arising out of" is a broad phrase that can only be interpreted in a broad manner. "This broad interpretation of 'arising out of' applies to both coverage provisions and exclusions." (The Travelers Property Casualty Co. of America v. Actavis, Inc. (2017) 16 Cal.App.5th 1026, 1045.) Numerous cases have applied an expansive definition of "arising out of" in the context of an exclusion. (See Crown Capital Securities, L.P. v. Endurance American Specialty Ins. Co. (2015) 235 Cal.App.4th 1122, 1131 [applying definition to policy exclusion]; Jon Davler, Inc. v. Arch Ins. Co. (2014) 229 Cal.App.4th 1025, 1035-1036 [applying definition to policy exclusion]; Southgate Recreation & Park Dist. v. California Assn. for Park & Recreation Ins. (2003) 106 Cal.App.4th 293, 300 [arising out of " 'broadly links' the exclusionary operative events with the exclusion" and is "generally equated" with " 'origination, growth or flow from the event' "]; Medill v. Westport Ins. Corp. (2006) 143 Cal.App.4th 819, 829-830 [broad interpretation of the term "arising out of" applies to breach of contract exclusion]; Aloha Pacific, Inc. v. California Ins. Guarantee Assn. (2000) 79 Cal.App.4th 297, 318-319 [broad interpretation given to term " 'arising out of' " in trademark exclusion in general liability insurance policy]; Fibreboard Corp. v. Hartford Accident & Indemnity Co. (1993) 16 Cal.App.4th 492, 503["California courts generally have given the term 'arising out of' or 'arising from' their commonsense meaning, concluding that they connote more than mere causation"]; see Trenches, Inc. v. Hanover Ins. Co. (9th Cir. 2014) 575 Fed.Appx. 741, 751 ["In California, the phrase 'arising out of' is construed broadly, even if in an exclusion"].) (Tentative Ruling Continues in Part 2 of 2) =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: MUSA JARADIE VS. YIHANG ZHANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion For An Order Imposing Terminating Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declaration Of Catherine Walsh In Support
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 23, 2019, Line 4, DEFENDANT YIHANG ZHANG's Motion For An Order Imposing Terminating Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Aaron Minnis, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. No opposition filed. The court is inclined to grant the motion and dismiss Plaintiff's complaint without prejudice pursuant to CCP 2023.030(d)(3) for failure to comply with the court's orders dated March 5, 2019 and June 10, 2019. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to aaron@minnisandsmallets.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SAMER NASSER ET AL VS. LISA MICHELLE MCCORMICK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, August 23, 2019, Line 10, DEFENDANTS PAUL STEELY, MICHELLE MCCORMICK, ESTATE OF ROBERT MCCORMICK, DECEASED'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. CCP Sec. 430.41 requires that counsel meet and confer "in person or by telephone" before filing a demurrer. Counsel's declaration indicates this did not occur, so the demurrer is ordered off calendar until after it does. The parties are ordered immediately to meet and confer and defendants have until September 20, 2019 to respond to the second amended complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: MASHA LEVINSON ET AL VS. BRIAN P. MASCHLER ESQ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, August 23, 2019, Line 11, CROSS-DEFENDANTS MASHA LEVINSON AND LEON TAYLOR'S Motion To Set Aside Default. Plaintiffs' motion to vacate the default entered on March 11, 2019 on the cross-complaint of Carlson, Calladine & Peterson LLP is granted. Plaintiff demonstrates that the default was the result of attorney mistake and therefore relief is mandatory. (See Code of Civ. Proc., Sec. 473(b); Sullivan Dec., Paras. 3, 12, 14, 16-18.) Mr. Sullivan shall remit $3,000 as reasonable attorney's fees to Carlson, Calladine & Peterson LLP by September 23, 2019. Those fees are reasonable for cross-complainant's efforts in obtaining the default and default judgment. Plaintiff shall file her answer by August 30, 2019. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: SHEREENA ABDULLAH VS. BAYVIEW HUNTER'S POINT MULTI-PURPOSE SENIOR ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Attendance At Her Deposition And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And Or Her Counsel Of Record
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 23, 2019, Line 5, DEFENDANT BAYVIEW HUNTERS POINT MULTIPURPOSE SENIOR's Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Attendance At Her Deposition And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And Or Her Counsel Of Record. Pro Tem Judge Aaron Minnis, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. Plaintiff has not provided the court with medical documentation to substantiate her contention that she is unable to have her deposition taken with or without accommodation. Counsel for the parties shall promptly meet and confer about date(s) for plaintiff's deposition and any reasonable accommodations that plaintiff may require. The accommodations sought shall be supported by a certification of plaintiff's health care provider which plaintiff's counsel shall provide to defense counsel prior to the deposition. The parties also shall meet and confer about a stipulated protective order to address plaintiff's privacy concerns. The deposition shall be completed by October 31, 2019. Sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to aaron@minnisandsmallets.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BATROA PROFIT SHARING PLAN ET AL VS. DEMETRIOS DIMITRIOU
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Written Responses To Rpds And Production Of Documents, Deem Admitted Requests For Admissions, And Compel Answers To Form Interrogatories From Dimitriou And Associates, P.C. And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 23, 2019, Line 6, PLAINTIFF BATROA PROFIT SHARING PLAN, MICHAEL LEVINE's Motion To Compel Written Responses To Rpds And Production Of Documents, Deem Admitted Requests For Admissions, And Compel Answers To Form Interrogatories From Dimitriou And Associates, P.C. And For Sanctions. Off calendar. Request for dismissal filed August 13, 2019.=(302/JPT)
